title: Permissions & Access Control

classes.name: PermissionService
methods: check(actor: Actor, permission_options: string|string[], scan_options?): Promise<boolean>
scan(actor: Actor, permission_options: string|string[], _reserved?, state?, scan_options?): Promise<any[]>
grant_user_app_permission(actor, app_uid, permission, extra?, meta?): Promise<void>
grant_dev_app_permission(actor, app_uid, permission, extra?, meta?): Promise<void>
grant_user_user_permission(actor, username: string, permission, extra?, meta?): Promise<void>
grant_user_group_permission(actor, gid, permission, extra?, meta?): Promise<void>

classes.name: ACLService
methods: check(actor: Actor, resource: FSNode|object, mode: string): Promise<boolean>
_check_fsNode(actor: Actor, fsNode: FSNode, mode: string): Promise<boolean>
checkResource(actor: Actor, resource: object, mode: string): Promise<boolean>
set_user_user(issuer: Actor, holder: Actor|string, resource: FSNode|string, mode: string, options?): Promise<boolean>
stat_user_user(issuer: Actor, holder: Actor, resource: FSNode): Promise<object>
classes.notes: Routes: POST /acl/stat-user-user, POST /acl/set-user-user. Mode hierarchy: see < list < read < write < manage.

classes.name: GroupService
methods: add_users({ uid: string, users: string[] }): Promise<void>
remove_users({ uid: string, users: string[] }): Promise<void>
classes.notes: Rate limit: max 20 group creations per owner per hour. list_public_groups() uses Redis cache (TTL 60s) via GroupRedisCacheSpace.

classes.name: VirtualGroupService
classes.notes: Only manages virtual (non-persistent) groups. Each implicator.run({ actor }) returns group IDs the actor belongs to.

classes.name: PermissionShortcutService
classes.notes: _init registers a shortcut implicator on PermissionService for 'service:puter-kvstore:ii:puter-kvstore'. Policy: rate-limit max 200 per 10000ms.

classes.name: GroupEntity
classes.notes: Created by create_group_entity(svc_group, values). Lazily fetches members via GroupService.list_members.

classes.name: PermissionImplicator
methods: create({ id, shortcut?, matcher, checker }): PermissionImplicator

key_relationships: ACLService depends_on PermissionService: scan(), grant_user_user_permission(), revoke_user_user_permission(), query_issuer_holder_permissions_by_prefix()
ACLService depends_on DatabaseService: direct DB reads for AccessToken permissions
PermissionService depends_on DatabaseService: read/write permission tables
PermissionService depends_on KVStore/Redis: permission scan cache (TTL 20s)
GroupService depends_on DatabaseService: CRUD on group/jct_user_group tables
GroupService depends_on Redis: public group cache (GroupRedisCacheSpace, TTL 60s)
GroupEntity composes GroupService: lazy member loading via list_members()
VirtualGroupService aggregates membership_implicators: runs them to compute virtual group membership
PermissionService uses VirtualGroupService: get_virtual_groups() during scan (via scan-permission.mjs)
PermissionService uses GroupService: group-based permission lookups during scan
actor_types: AppUnderUserActorType - app acting on behalf of user
acl_check_logic: 3. UserActor accessing own home dir → short-circuit true
4. AppUnderUserActor accessing own AppData → short-circuit true
5. Public folder check (if enabled) → read/list/see allowed if owner email confirmed
6. AccessTokenActor → check authorizer has perm AND token has perm in DB
7. AppUnderUserActor → check user also has perm first
8. AppUnderUserActor cross-user AppData → allow if user has shared perm
9. General → walk directory tree upward, call PermissionService.scan() at each node
important_notes: Permission scan uses Redis cache (TTL 20s) keyed by actor UID + permission. scan-permission.mjs external sequence handles full scan logic with implicators/exploders. grant_user_user_permission fires both flat (sync) and linked (async) DB writes. Hardcoded group permissions loaded from hardcoded-permissions.js and augmented via 'create.permissions' event.
